item.title: Re: Picture Credits
item.content:encoded: With no indication these images have been released under Creative Commons, the images are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm on the fence as to whether or not to upvote this. My feelings on the interview keep flip-flopping between "this was too in your face" and "that final interaction justifies everything before". I'm not voting now, but it's a solid article overall.

item.title: Consider Anti-Memes
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this one. The containment procedure is very unique. My only suggestion is that you add the tag anti-meme since SCP-3433 seems to possess Anti-memetic effects.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Make it more obvious that the researcher is being affected by the anomalous effect. Not sure how I would do that, personally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If this is your second SCP I'm excited what will come in the future! The only thing that bugged me was the ending statement by the Doctor during the interview. It seems really out of place for the tone and was given no reason to be prompted.

+1

item.title: Re: Sorry :D
item.content:encoded: Special Containment Procedures are meant to be something that a Researcher or whoever can look at in an emergency and, with no prior knowledge of the object, work out what they need to do to contain it. If you have a bunch of redacted information, that kind of goes out the window.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the placid resignation of the Superstar in the interview. "Yes, I had my fifteen minutes. Now it's over, and that's just how it's supposed to be."

By the by, I only learned recently just how badly injured Warhol had been in the shooting, and how long and incomplete his recovery was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So make the effect less obvious? ("less subtlety" — do you mean more subtle?) (or am I reading this wrong?)

item.title: Picture Credits
item.content:encoded:
Sorry about this, I forgot to credit my pictures!

"The Factory" - http://www.cnn.com/style/article/andy-warhol-factory-stephen-shore/index.html?gallery=%2F%2Fcdn.cnn.com%2Fcnnnext%2Fdam%2Fassets%2F161102170233-new-andy-warhol-factory-stephen-shore-6.jpg

"Andy Warhol" - http://www.cnn.com/style/article/andy-warhol-factory-stephen-shore/index.html?gallery=%2F%2Fcdn.cnn.com%2Fcnnnext%2Fdam%2Fassets%2F161102161906-new-andy-warhol-factory-stephen-shore-1.jpg

"The Star" - https://guyhepner.com/product/the-star-by-andy-warhol/

"Skull 158" - https://guyhepner.com/product/skull-158-by-andy-warhol/

"The Shadow" - https://guyhepner.com/product/the-shadow-by-andy-warhol/

"Warhol-Superstar-2-Interview" - http://www.cnn.com/style/article/andy-warhol-factory-stephen-shore/index.html?gallery=%2F%2Fcdn.cnn.com%2Fcnnnext%2Fdam%2Fassets%2F161102162046-new-andy-warhol-factory-stephen-shore-2.jpg

"Warhol-Superstar-1" - http://www.nydailynews.com/entertainment/remembering-life-legacy-andy-warhol-gallery-1.1893857?pmSlide=1.1893848

item.title: Metric Units
item.content:encoded: Ok, give me like 10 seconds, I'll change everything to metric units.

item.title: Sorry :D
item.content:encoded:
1. Sorry about the interview. It was a bit rough, I'll admit. I'm trying to improve on that.

2. I don't think I was clear enough about the effects. So, the effects are permanent, but there won't be any further effects as long as the ownership changes (ex. memories will remain erased, but SCP-3433-n-n won't disappear as long as ownership changes before then).

3. I'm sorry, but what's the problem with the [REDACTED] in the containment? I thought I was just trying to take out the names of all the researchers?

Sorry about that. I'll keep that in mind next time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two quick things:

All artwork/paintings related to SCP-3433 is to be

  • Since this is affecting multiple paintings/pieces of art, I think it should be "…SCP-3433 are to be…"

approximately 50 x 50 x 3"

  • Not metric units

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the ideas here are really cool, I like the concept of the Foundation being in possession of something that, if ownership isnt regularly cycled, will cause their employees to completely forget them.

There are a lot of issues though, starting with imperial measures (should be metric) and a [REDACTED] in the containment procedures, which is normally enough for an instant downvote from me.
You say that the effects of the painting are permanent, unless ownership of the paintings changes, which means the effects ARENT permanent, and yet the two missing superstars whose paintings the Foundation owns don't seem to have reappeared.

Aaannnd, sorry, but that interview log was pretty painful to read. The Doctor just seemed to be wilfully ignorant to the point of farce. Everyone knows the whole "15 minutes of fame" deal, it's not a difficult concept, and he seemed to be ignorant of the fact that it was even an anomaly (demanding what the superstar means and how it works, etc.)

Oh, and the numbering system, with n-n and all that is unnecessarily complicated. Seems like different paintings all have the same effect, so you could simplify that a lot - no reason to have classifications specific to which person was affected by which painting. Seems like you posted this without getting any feedback first, which would have helped improve a lot of these issues.

Still, the ideas here are really good, so I'll leave it at a no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know nothing about Andy Warhol, but its inherent mistery was the perfect channel for the success of this article. TL;DR: weird as fuck buy totally enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

This is excellent for a second skip! Great job… I really appreciated all of the "historical" detail, and the primary effect was actually quite unnerving.

A question about the images, though, where did you get them? And are they CC-compliant?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally felt that this statement was a bit of the anomalous effect bleeding through into the interview. Maybe that could've been done with less subtlety.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do you suggest would be a better way to end this interview? How about interviews in general?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only real issue here is that the Doctor's parting comment in the interview is really on the nose. Not to mention it seemingly comes out of nowhere. Had the interviewee said something just prior to it that would have prompted that comment, perhaps it wouldn't have stood out so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was on the fence as a draft - all the changes you made were perfect imo, and you really tightened it up into a solid article. Even as someone who's not a big fan of Warhol or art history in general, I had a fully enjoyable time reading this; that's hard to pull off with an article so full of biographical information. I'm sure this is going to stick around, and I'm excited to see what you write next.

item.title: I am a Deeply Superficial Person
item.content:encoded: Hello! This is my second attempt at an SCP (following the failure of a first), and I've tried my best to improve my writing! Thanks for taking the time to read! I hope I can continue to improve in the future!
